top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY San Juan's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
GFNY San Juan's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
GFNY San Juan's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 96 miles.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
At mile 60 of 96, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY San Juan. Train it until it feels boring.
At GFNY San Juan, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY San Juan, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 96 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,265ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY San Juan’s 96 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY San Juan. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY San Juan requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, TT Threshold, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 96 miles of mixed terrain in San Juan, Argentina.
